response.justification: The candidate has peripheral relevance through GIS/geospatial platform work and some experience with high-throughput distributed systems, but the core technical requirements are largely unmet. The role demands deep modern C++ or Rust expertise for routing data pipelines, and the candidate's primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js. The only C/C++ experience listed is from National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project—both over a decade old and not in the routing/navigation domain. The geospatial work (GISCollective, Green Map) is focused on data collection and visualization rather than routing tile generation or navigation pipeline processing.
strengths: Geospatial and GIS domain familiarity through GISCollective and Green Map projects, including a FOSS4G conference talk
Demonstrated experience with high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby using async I/O and high-performance databases)
Broad systems breadth including Linux, embedded systems, robotics, and Kubernetes-based infrastructure
concerns: No demonstrable modern C++ or Rust proficiency; primary language stack is TypeScript/Node.js/JavaScript throughout recent career
C/C++ exposure is over a decade old (National Instruments 2014–2015, bachelor thesis) with no evidence of continued use or depth
No experience with routing tile generation, navigation data pipelines, or real-time traffic/map data integration at any level
